beats.activity: Wake up, shower, coffee, and light breakfast at home.
beats.reasoning: Starting the day at home in Renton. Yesterday ended at -122.198334, 47.454044 (Bagel Store area), so River returned home overnight. Morning routine is compact given the drizzly weather and an energy level at 50/100 suggests a slower, more deliberate start.

beats.activity: Commute to Bellevue, taking I-405 south to avoid SR-520 eastbound closure (9am–noon).
beats.reasoning: SR-520 eastbound is closed 9am–noon, so River leaves early (7:50am) to beat the closure and avoid cross-lake snarl. I-405 is the safer alternate route from Renton to Bellevue. Budgeting 55 min for a normally 40-min drive due to rain and early-morning caution.

beats.activity: Sprint planning, design reviews, and roadmap work in the office.
beats.reasoning: Typical Tuesday morning and early afternoon at the fintech startup office. The rain is steady until 2pm, so staying indoors makes sense. Work block ends before lunch to allow for a midday break.

beats.activity: Lunch at a cozy bagel spot downtown Bellevue, staying dry from the rain.
beats.reasoning: River has strong opinions about bagels and is a regular at bagel shops. With steady rain until 2pm, grabbing lunch indoors at a nearby bagel spot in downtown Bellevue is pragmatic and on-brand. Quick errand-style meal, 45 min total.

beats.activity: Return to office: stakeholder meetings, roadmap refinement, and wrap-up work.
beats.reasoning: Afternoon work block. Rain is clearing by 2pm, so River stays productive in the office. Standard fintech PM afternoon of meetings and planning. Ending at 5:30pm allows for a timely commute home.

beats.activity: Commute home from Bellevue to Renton via I-405, now clear of morning closure.
beats.reasoning: SR-520 closure has ended (noon), so I-405 is flowing normally. Evening commute is slightly slower due to post-work traffic and lingering drizzle. Budgeting 55 min to account for typical evening congestion.

beats.activity: Dinner prep and eat at home—simple comfort food.
beats.reasoning: After a long day commuting around the SR-520 closure, River opts for a quiet home-cooked dinner. Pragmatic choice given the drizzly weather and moderate energy level (50/100). Settles in for the evening.

diary:
Woke up to that grey Seattle drizzle—the kind that makes you want to stay under the covers. Got my coffee down and headed out early, knowing the SR-520 mess would tank the usual commute. Took I-405 south instead, which felt smart until I realized everyone else had the same idea. Still beat the worst of it.

Spent the morning in sprint planning with the team, hammering out roadmap priorities. My brain was foggy until I ducked out for lunch at Blazer Bagels around 1pm. Something about getting out of the office and eating a real bagel—not the sad desk lunch—shifted my whole mood. The rain had finally stopped by then.

Afternoon was stakeholder meetings, the usual dance of managing expectations and timelines. By 7pm, driving home on the now-clear I-405, I felt oddly satisfied. Made pasta, sat on the couch. Simple day, but it landed right.
thought_process: The bridge closure threw my morning plans sideways, but I'd actually checked the traffic app before leaving—something I don't always do. That small bit of preparation kept me calm instead of frustrated. I think I'm learning to treat these Seattle commute surprises as puzzles rather than disasters. The afternoon felt long, but getting outside for lunch was non-negotiable; I've noticed my mood tanks when I'm trapped at my desk. By evening, the roads cleared and so did my headspace. Nothing dramatic happened today, but the small wins—routing around traffic, a good bagel, wrapping work on time—accumulated into a sense of control I needed.
world_event_prompt: Grey, drizzly Tuesday with steady rain from 7am to 2pm, high of 58F. SR-520 bridge closed eastbound 9am-noon for emergency repair, snarling cross-lake traffic.
